notes: The candidate works. The only minor repairs are to stipulate that relations are false outside intended sorts and, in the copy-computation algorithm, to search from both hardwired roots of a toggled gadget unless side information is also hardwired.

task_title: Adversarial audit of the candidate construction
notes: No concrete obstruction was found. After making the language and arbitrary-copy algorithm explicit and strengthening the shift argument to a strong self-embedding argument, the proposed construction appears to establish the intended diagonal separation.

The non-Sigma^in_1 part is robust. Parameters from a union pi(a), including U-parameters that reveal the fresh label and side information via T, do not defeat the shift argument. The proof depends only on choosing l so that no parameter lies inside the gadget P_l.

task_title: Independent audit of the full construction
notes: No remaining flaw was found in the repaired construction. With I included, all off-sort relations declared false, and the shift argument stated as a strong self-embedding argument, the construction verifies the desired properties.

task_title: Focused formalization of computable AUT-countability on arbitrary copies
notes: The arbitrary-copy computation is fully formal under the usual nonuniform Turing reducibility convention. No additional cone base is needed; the only caveat is that stronger uniformity or c.e.-enumeration-only conventions would require different arguments or are not part of the stated definition.

task_title: Final adversarial referee audit of the proposed positive construction
notes: No fatal gap was found. The final write-up should explicitly state the nonuniform Turing-reducibility convention, that the oracle is a deciding atomic diagram, that U in arbitrary copies is only a torsor, and that Sigma-in-1 is being used in the standard existential sense.

task_title: Final syntax and definition audit of the positive construction
notes: No fatal gap was found. The final proof should explicitly state the complete atomic diagram convention, nonuniform Turing reducibility, finite hardwiring of support data, off-sort falsity, and the standard existential meaning of Sigma-in-1.
